summary:
Routine chest radiography after transbronchial biopsy is not indicated because chest CT provides superior sensitivity and detail compared to standard chest X-rays for detecting thoracic pathologies. The low resolution and limited ability of plain films to detect subtle findings make them inadequate follow-up tools post-procedure. 

Final Answer: No
